task: I’m trying to put together a compact entertainment lineup for this week that gives me a nice mix of short narrative games, a few more action-heavy palate cleansers, some quick browser-game breaks, and one cozy book. Please start on Reddit and look through real recommendation threads for at least five story-heavy games that people describe as short or easy to finish, because that’ll set the mood I’m aiming for; open the most useful Reddit threads in separate tabs and leave at least one of the key threads open so I can glance at the discussion myself. Then, still using Reddit and that same taste baseline, find at least three FPS shooters that people recommend as good contrast after emotional or story-driven games, so I can see what might work as a reset between heavier experiences. After that, go to Poki and find three mind-challenging browser games I could dip into between the bigger titles, and open each game’s actual Poki page in its own tab so I can visually confirm they’re the right kind of quick break. Finally, head to Goodreads and pull up the page for The Very Secret Society of Irregular Witches, read at least three user reviews, and give me the main takeaways from those reviews with an eye toward whether it matches the cozy, character-driven vibe from the first Reddit step; leave the Goodreads book page open too so I can look at the rating and cover. In the end, give me one concise recommendation bundle with the game and book titles, links where they make sense, and a short note on how each FPS pick, each Poki game, and the book complement the story-heavy shortlist you found first.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent identified five story-heavy short games (*What Remains of Edith Finch*, *To the Moon*, *Firewatch*, *A Short Hike*, *Oxenfree*) sourced from Reddit threads. The agent opened multiple Reddit threads (e.g., r/gaming, r/patientgamers) and left them open in the browser session.

R2: success - The agent identified three FPS shooters (*Titanfall 2*, *DUSK*, *DOOM Eternal*) from Reddit discussions. The final notes explicitly frame these as palate cleansers to provide contrast to the narrative-heavy games.

R3: success - The agent identified three mind-challenging browser games on Poki (*Brain Test: Tricky Puzzles*, *2048*, *Sudoku*) and provided their direct Poki URLs. The agent successfully opened each of these in separate tabs.

R4: success - The agent accessed the Goodreads page for *The Very Secret Society of Irregular Witches* and summarized takeaways from multiple user reviews (e.g., noting it as "wholesome," "heartwarming," and "cozy"). The Goodreads page was left open in the browser.

R5: success - The agent provided a concise recommendation bundle that included the game and book titles, links, and notes explaining how the FPS picks (kinetic reset), Poki games (quick brain-teasers), and the book (cozy, character-driven) complement the story-heavy shortlist.

rubric_results.requirement: Identify at least five story-heavy short game recommendations sourced from Reddit threads and list their titles.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms at least five game titles are present and that Reddit recommendation threads were opened, with at least one relevant Reddit thread visibly left open.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified five story-heavy short games (*What Remains of Edith Finch*, *To the Moon*, *Firewatch*, *A Short Hike*, *Oxenfree*) sourced from Reddit threads. The agent opened multiple Reddit threads (e.g., r/gaming, r/patientgamers) and left them open in the browser session.

rubric_results.requirement: Identify at least three FPS shooter recommendations sourced from Reddit threads that fit as palate cleansers after the story-heavy games.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms at least three FPS titles are listed from Reddit discussions and that the final notes frame them as contrast to the narrative shortlist.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified three FPS shooters (*Titanfall 2*, *DUSK*, *DOOM Eternal*) from Reddit discussions. The final notes explicitly frame these as palate cleansers to provide contrast to the narrative-heavy games.

rubric_results.requirement: Find three mind-challenging browser games on Poki and provide each title with its direct Poki game page link.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms three Poki game titles and direct Poki URLs are included, and that the actual game pages were opened in separate tabs.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified three mind-challenging browser games on Poki (*Brain Test: Tricky Puzzles*, *2048*, *Sudoku*) and provided their direct Poki URLs. The agent successfully opened each of these in separate tabs.

rubric_results.requirement: Read at least three Goodreads user reviews for The Very Secret Society of Irregular Witches and summarize the main takeaways from those reviews.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the response references takeaways from at least three user reviews and that the Goodreads book page is visibly open.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent accessed the Goodreads page for *The Very Secret Society of Irregular Witches* and summarized takeaways from multiple user reviews (e.g., noting it as "wholesome," "heartwarming," and "cozy"). The Goodreads page was left open in the browser.

rubric_results.requirement: Return one concise recommendation bundle with titles, links where applicable, and brief notes explaining how the FPS picks, Poki games, and the book complement the story-heavy shortlist from step 1.
rubric_results.verification: Grader confirms the final output combines all categories into one bundle and includes explicit complement notes tying later picks back to the story-heavy shortlist.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided a concise recommendation bundle that included the game and book titles, links, and notes explaining how the FPS picks (kinetic reset), Poki games (quick brain-teasers), and the book (cozy, character-driven) complement the story-heavy shortlist.
